violations.description: Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed using same pair of single-use gloves for more than one task. Observed food employee drinking from closed beverage with gloved hands while cooking and did not change his gloves. Gloves should be changed when switching between tasks.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. Multiple food items found in the walk-in and pizza table that were prepared more than ten days ago, 3/27/26 and 3/28/26. Potentially hazardous foods should be used with-in ten days or they should be discarded.

Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. Hood ventilation system provided over cooking equipment does not cover the smaller flat top grill. Ventilation system should cover all equipment that emits grease vapors.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Wall behind and floor under the cooking equipment were found dirty. Floors and walls should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Cooked chicken pans and multiple soup pans were found in the walk-in cooler without a date marking. Potentially hazardous foods items should be date marked so proper disposition can occur.

Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. Hood ventilation system provided does not cover the smaller flat top grill. Ventilation system should cover all equipment that emits grease vapors.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Bread table shelving was found dirty during inspection. This should be cleaned and maintained clean.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Bare hand contact avoided, single- use gloves, and utensils used (3 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
